privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How theRec collects, uses, and safeguards your workspace data on our screen recording and video collaboration platform.
privacy.s1Body: We store core identity profiles such as your authenticated name, primary email address, and security credentials. To properly execute workflows across our infrastructure, we also capture dynamic video metadata, active workspace parameters, historical subscription statuses, and aggregate user interaction events.
privacy.s2Body: Your telemetry and data feeds are applied explicitly to maintain browser-level recording captures, optimize automated encoding queues, route real-time workspace collaborations, settle subscription billing, emit critical event notifications, prevent automated platform abuse, and iterate code performance over time.
privacy.s3BodyPrefix: We operate under a strict baseline:
privacy.s3BodyStrong: we do not sell your personal files or telemetry.
privacy.s3BodySuffix: Data is strictly exposed to trusted third-party cloud infrastructure processors tasked with critical tasks like asset hosting, core storage replication, product analytics pipelines, and secure transaction handling.
privacy.s4Body: Active workspace matrices are kept intact while your root account is marked open. When a video is intentionally sent to the trash container, our system maintains it for a temporary safety window to prevent accidental loss prior to running permanent database deletion cycles.
privacy.s5Intro: theRec.site uses modern security measures and technologies to help protect data in transit and at rest, including:
privacy.s5Footnote: Despite strong safeguards, no internet-connected system can be guaranteed 100% secure.
privacy.s6BodyPrefix: To process structural data extractions, account erasures, or specialized privacy requests, route a request directly through our digital
privacy.s7Intro: theRec.site is designed for screen recording, video hosting, sharing, and team collaboration. Users should not upload, publish, or share highly sensitive information unless they are legally authorized to do so.
privacy.s7ExamplesLead: Examples of content you should avoid uploading without proper authorization include:
privacy.s7Example2: Financial records or payment card information
privacy.s7Example5: Personal information belonging to third parties
privacy.s7Action: If such content creates privacy, security, legal, or compliance concerns, theRec.site may remove or restrict access to the content without prior notice.
privacy.s8Body: theRec.site is not obligated to monitor all uploaded content, but reserves the right to review, investigate, restrict, remove, or disable access to any content that may violate laws, policies, intellectual property rights, privacy rights, or platform security requirements.
privacy.s9Body: theRec.site may comply with valid legal requests, court orders, regulatory obligations, and law enforcement requirements when legally required to do so, including disclosure of account or content information where permitted by applicable law.
privacy.s10Intro: Paid subscription terms, refund eligibility, and cancellation rules are described in our dedicated

terms.title: Terms & Conditions
terms.subtitle: Clear policies and fair rules for browser-based screen recording, video hosting, sharing, and collaboration on theRec.site.
terms.introP1: Welcome to theRec.site, an independently operated software-as-a-service (SaaS) platform for browser-based screen recording, video hosting, video sharing, and asynchronous collaboration.
terms.introP2: These Terms & Conditions explain how to use the platform responsibly, outline your rights, and describe how subscriptions and services are managed — so everyone has a clear and fair experience.
terms.introP3: By using the platform or creating an account on theRec.site, you agree to follow these Terms and the related policies, including our Privacy Policy, Copyright Policy, Abuse Reporting Policy, and Refund Policy.
terms.introP4: theRec.site is independently operated and maintained by its founder. The platform is not a marketplace, social network, or public content hosting service. Its primary purpose is private screen recording, controlled video sharing, and team collaboration.
terms.s1P1: theRec.site may only be used for lawful, legitimate purposes and in compliance with applicable laws and regulations.
terms.s1P2: Account holders are fully responsible for all activity under their account, including:
terms.s1Responsibility5: Content posted or distributed through the platform
terms.s1ProhibitedHeading: You may not use the platform for any of the following:
terms.s1Prohibited1: Publishing or sharing malware or disruptive content
terms.s1Prohibited3: Uploading or sharing illegal or abusive content
terms.s1Prohibited4: Misusing storage resources or service infrastructure
terms.s1Prohibited5: Attempting unauthorized access to the system or user data
terms.s1Footnote: theRec.site may suspend, restrict, or terminate accounts that violate these policies or affect platform stability and security. See our Abuse Reporting Policy for additional detail.
terms.s2P1: All recordings, files, and content you upload or create through theRec.site remain entirely yours.
terms.s2P2: The platform does not claim ownership of your content. Data is used only as needed to run the service and improve the experience, such as:
terms.s2P3: theRec.site does not sell your content, monetize it, or share it with third parties.
terms.s2SensitiveIntro: You should not upload, publish, or share highly sensitive information unless you are legally authorized to do so.
terms.s2Sensitive2: Financial records or payment card information
terms.s2Sensitive5: Personal information belonging to third parties
terms.s2SensitiveAction: If such content creates privacy, security, legal, or compliance concerns, theRec.site may remove or restrict access to the content without prior notice. You remain responsible for the legality of content you upload.
terms.s3P1: The platform provides tools to share recordings and organize collaboration within workspaces.
terms.s3Admin3: Controlling link visibility and content access
terms.s3P2: When you set a recording to "Public" or "Anyone with the link," you understand that it may become accessible to people outside your workspace.
terms.s3Warning: Always review sharing settings before publishing any recording that contains private or sensitive information.
terms.s4P1: theRec.site offers free and paid plans with different storage limits and features. Refund eligibility is governed by our
terms.s4UpgradeBody: When you upgrade to a higher plan, new features take effect immediately. Any price difference may be charged on a prorated basis for the remainder of your current billing cycle.
terms.s4DowngradeP1: When you move to a lower plan, your current benefits continue until the end of the paid period. New plan limits apply automatically at the next renewal.
terms.s4RenewalsBody: Paid subscriptions renew automatically unless you cancel before the next renewal date in your account or billing settings. Cancellation stops future renewals only; unless a refund is approved, access to paid features remains active until the end of the current billing period.
terms.s4PaymentNote: All payments are processed securely through trusted external payment providers. theRec.site does not store card details directly on its servers.
terms.s5P1: We work to keep the service stable and fast, with ongoing improvements to infrastructure and the platform.
terms.s5P2: However, temporary outages, maintenance, or technical issues beyond our control may occur, including:
terms.s5Footnote: Although we work continuously to minimize downtime, uninterrupted service at all times cannot be guaranteed.
terms.s6P1: theRec.site is not obligated to monitor all uploaded content, but reserves the right to review, investigate, restrict, remove, or disable access to any content that may violate laws, policies, intellectual property rights, privacy rights, or platform security requirements.
terms.s6P2: When necessary to protect users, legal compliance, or platform integrity, content may be removed, public access disabled, or accounts suspended or permanently terminated. No prior notice is required when immediate action is necessary.
terms.s7Body: theRec.site may comply with valid legal requests, court orders, regulatory obligations, and law enforcement requirements when legally required to do so, including preservation or disclosure of information associated with accounts or content where permitted by applicable law.
terms.s8P1: theRec.site services are provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis.
terms.s8P2: The platform is not liable for indirect losses or damages resulting from:
terms.s8Footnote: We recommend keeping local backups of important recordings or files.
terms.s9Intro: These Terms work together with our other published policies:
terms.s9Privacy: Privacy Policy — data collection, security, and privacy requests
terms.s9Copyright: Copyright Policy — intellectual property and infringement
terms.s9Abuse: Abuse Reporting Policy — content safety and enforcement
terms.s9Refund: Refund Policy — subscription refunds and cancellations
terms.closingP1: These Terms are not meant to be legal complexity for its own sake. They provide a clear, fair framework for a stable, secure, and easy-to-use experience for everyone.
terms.closing4: Respect for users' privacy and their content

copyright.title: Copyright & Intellectual Property Policy
copyright.subtitle: How theRec.site respects intellectual property rights and handles copyright complaints.
copyright.s1P1: Users may only upload content they own or have legal rights to use, including recordings, files, and other materials shared through theRec.site.
copyright.s1P2: Uploading copyrighted content without authorization from the rights holder is strictly prohibited. You remain fully responsible for the legality of all content you upload, publish, or distribute through the platform.
copyright.s2Body: theRec.site respects intellectual property rights and expects users to do the same. We respond to valid copyright complaints submitted in good faith with sufficient information to identify the reported material.
copyright.s3P1: If the platform receives a legitimate copyright infringement notice, the reported content may be disabled, restricted, or permanently removed without prior notice.
copyright.s3P2: To report alleged infringement, contact us through our Contact page with details identifying the content, your relationship to the rights holder, and a good-faith statement of unauthorized use.
copyright.s4Body: Users who repeatedly upload or share infringing content may have their accounts suspended or permanently terminated, in addition to removal of the reported material.
copyright.s5Body: theRec.site is not obligated to proactively monitor all user uploads, but reserves the right to review, investigate, and take action on content that may violate copyright law or this policy.

abuse.title: Abuse Reporting & Content Safety Policy
abuse.subtitle: How theRec.site investigates abuse reports and protects users, compliance, and platform integrity.
abuse.s1Intro: theRec.site may investigate reports and take action against content or activity involving:
abuse.s1Item6: Sensitive personal information published without authorization
abuse.s2Intro: When necessary to protect users, legal compliance, or platform integrity, theRec.site reserves the right to:
abuse.s2Footnote: No prior notice is required when immediate action is necessary to address serious risk, illegal activity, or security threats.
abuse.s3Body: If you believe content on theRec.site violates this policy or applicable law, contact us through our Contact page with a description of the issue, relevant links or identifiers, and any supporting information. We review reports in good faith and may request additional details.
abuse.s4Body: theRec.site is not obligated to monitor all uploaded content, but reserves the right to review, investigate, restrict, remove, or disable access to any content that may violate laws, policies, intellectual property rights, privacy rights, or platform security requirements.

refund.title: Refund Policy
refund.subtitle: Subscription refund eligibility, cancellation rules, and how requests are evaluated on theRec.site.
refund.s1Body: theRec.site offers free and paid subscription plans with different features and usage limits. This Refund Policy explains when refunds may be available and how cancellation affects your access.
refund.s2Body: Monthly subscriptions are generally non-refundable once a billing cycle has started.
refund.s3P1: Annual subscribers may request a refund within seven (7) calendar days from the payment date.
refund.s3P2: Refunds are only available if a substantial portion of the plan's included resources has not been consumed during the refund eligibility period.
refund.s3ThresholdLead: For the purpose of this policy, usage is considered substantial when the customer has consumed twenty-five percent (25%) or more of any primary plan allowance or usage limit, including but not limited to:
refund.s3Allowance1: Number of recordings or videos included in the plan
refund.s3Allowance5: Any other metered or usage-based platform resources
refund.s3Ineligible: If usage reaches or exceeds twenty-five percent (25%) of any primary plan allowance, the refund request may be deemed ineligible.
refund.s3ExampleBody: If a plan includes up to 60 video uploads, uploading 15 or more videos during the refund eligibility period may make the subscription ineligible for a refund.
refund.s3Review: All refund requests are reviewed based on actual platform usage data recorded by the system. To submit a request, contact support via the email on our Contact page within the eligible period.
refund.s4Body: Cancellation stops future renewals only. Unless a refund is approved, access to paid features remains available until the end of the current billing period.
